scheduled_followups.why: On 2026-05-01 bg_loop_silent (sev2) fired immediately on first deploy with loop=health_monitor, age_s=250. Investigation showed no real outage — the bug was a calibration drift: bg_heartbeat.EXPECTED_INTERVAL_S["health_monitor"]=60 vs actual asyncio.sleep(300) in health_monitor.py:101. Fix landed e820ca85. The same drift class will bite again as new loops are added or sleep cadences change. This task spawns an audit fork to catch all such drifts + recommend an AST-grep CI gate.
scheduled_followups.doc: CLAUDE.md Session 214 entry on bg_heartbeat calibration; commit e820ca85
scheduled_followups.action_prompt: Spawn a research fork: walk EXPECTED_INTERVAL_S in mcp-server/central-command/backend/bg_heartbeat.py + task_defs in mcp-server/main.py; for each entry, find the corresponding loop function and grep its inter-iteration asyncio.sleep(N); flag any drift > 50%. Also list loops in task_defs without EXPECTED_INTERVAL_S coverage. Recommend an AST-grep CI gate. Report under 600 words. Research-only — no code changes from the fork.

scheduled_followups.why: CronCreate scheduled cron e6242204 for Monday 8:43 AM local but the durable flag appears non-functional in this runtime build (response said Session-only despite durable=true). Without the cross-session pattern this entry, the digest stops the moment my session ends. Future-Claude on first invocation should: (1) run the digest fork itself, (2) re-schedule the cron with CronCreate("43 8 * * 1", recurring=true, durable=true), (3) bump THIS scheduled_followups entry to next Monday to keep the self-healing chain alive.

scheduled_followups.why: BUG 2 root cause was credentials: 'same-origin' on SiteComplianceHero.tsx:37 producing 401 → null → No Data on the dashboard. Fixed inline 2026-05-01. Repo grep found 63 OTHER sites with the same pattern (AuthContext, portal browser-verify, partner audit log, integrations API, multiple SiteX components, etc.). Round-table consensus: do NOT mass-rewrite without per-site verification. Some may LEGITIMATELY want 'same-origin' (e.g. portal browser-verify intentionally uses anonymous fetches for cryptographic isolation). Sweep MUST be per-site, eyes-on.
scheduled_followups.action_prompt: For each of the 63 sites in mcp-server/central-command/frontend/src/ matching `credentials: 'same-origin'`: (1) determine if the endpoint requires auth (read the route handler in backend); (2) if YES auth required → swap to 'include'; (3) if NO auth required (e.g. browser-verify, public portal home) → add `// same-origin-allowed: <reason>` opt-out marker on the line; (4) decrement BASELINE_MAX in test_no_same_origin_credentials.py by the swap count. Sites flagged by Diana for priority monitoring: SiteSLAIndicator.tsx:102, SiteActivityTimeline.tsx:113, SiteSearchBar.tsx:107.

Session 217 — client portal data-display architecture P0 + 4-stage closure. ROOT CAUSE: tenant_middleware.org_connection() set app.current_tenant='' which made every site-RLS query under client sessions return zero rows. Customer (relltherell@gmail) saw FOUR contradictory numbers on same org (top tile 20.8% / per-site 93% / Reports 100% / Evidence 0 of 155K bundles). Round-table 25 6/6 APPROVE_AFTER_DECISION. Stage 1 (2374f7c2 + 6f1fbcab): mig 278 adds tenant_org_isolation policy uniformly to 29 site-RLS tables via DO-block + STABLE rls_site_belongs_to_current_org() helper; honest score defaults (null + status no_data/partial/healthy) replacing 100% antipattern at 4 sites; frontend '—' / 'Awaiting first scan' UI; CI gate test_org_scoped_rls_policies.py prevents new site-RLS tables from missing the org policy. Stage 2 (add0b1cc + 08f28793): canonical compute_compliance_score(conn, site_ids, *, include_incidents) helper used by all 3 surfaces (dashboard/reports/per-site). Same algorithm everywhere: latest-per-(site,check_type,hostname). Verified on prod: 89.2% (338/379) for North Valley — same number on all surfaces. Agent compliance unblended from 70/30 mix and rendered as sibling tile. Data Freshness tile shows last_check_at + stale_check_count. Stage 4 (25a46a4a + d20e6793): client_portal_zero_evidence_with_data sev2 invariant + canonical runbook — simulates client RLS context for 10 most-recently-active orgs each loop, fires if any has bundles in last 7d but visible_count=0. Substrate invariant 55→56. Verified clean post-deploy. Bonus: P0 client-login restore (3be6d65b) — request_magic_link + login_with_password silently 500'd in prod for 26 days due to broken `from .rate_limiter import check_rate_limit`; module-level wrapper restored + AST CI gate test_lazy_import_resolution.py for the entire class. Plus pre-push eslint hardening (ab831e71). Round-table 22 design doc shipped under #23 (mig 277 — client_user email rename, 4 endpoints, ALLOWED_EVENTS 45→49, 38 tests). North Valley one-shot SQL recovery applied manually for testing access (relltherell@gmail.com owner inserted, jbouey@osiriscare.net retired with tombstone email).

Plus P0 hotfix: pre-push now compiles every backend .py through python3.11 to catch the f-string-with-backslash deploy class that bricked 3 RT33 deploys. Adversarial round-tables at gates 1+2 with Patricia (HIPAA counsel surrogate) + Marcus (regulatory engineer) + Linda (PM) — Marcus VETO on cooling-off CHECK + Marcus VETO on FK incompatibility for flag-flip (event dropped, audit lives in append-only feature_flags + admin_audit_log) + Patricia REQUEST CHANGES on multi-owner attribution (pinned expected_*_email columns) + Patricia REQUEST CHANGES on plaintext token leak (email-only delivery; tokens never returned). Maya final 2nd-eye APPROVE. Feature flag stays disabled until outside HIPAA counsel signs off on §164.504(e) BA-to-BA inapplicability + §164.528 disclosure-accounting under chain immutability + receiving-org BAA scope.
